problem_statement: Direct execution of LLM-generated code in production/agentic systems is unsafe—it can be unstable, erroneous, or contain vulnerabilities and malicious attacks—yet human code reviews and traditional secure testing tools are impractical or untrustworthy in headless, low-latency workflows.
magnitude_evidence: Meta's CodeShield reported a True Block Rate of 0.68 and True Allow Rate of 0.93 on InjectedHumanEval; dataset comprises 164 safe and 470 unsafe samples across a subset of CWE vulnerabilities
insufficiency_evidence: human code reviews and traditional secure testing tools are impractical or untrustworthy; CodeShield reported a TBR of 0.68 (missing ~32% of malicious samples) and offers limited security coverage and lacks configurable controls
solution_mechanisms: AST validation against safe grammar subset
safe code generation with injected controls
timeouts and retry with exponential backoff
runtime feedback loop to code-gen LLM
key_constructs: STELP (Secure Transpiler and Executor of LLM-Generated Programs)
notes: Some figures/tables (AST diagrams, code snippets) rendered as garbled OCR but core text is intact; human oversight is explicitly framed as impractical, so STELP is a fully automated mechanism (human_role none).
